reasoning: I will summarize the causes of pulmonary hypertension as listed in the provided content, organizing them into the standard five groups and noting a related Eisenmenger syndrome example.
output:
- Group 1: Pulmonary arterial hypertension
  - Idiopathic pulmonary arterial hypertension (cause unknown)
  - Heritable pulmonary arterial hypertension (gene mutations in families)
  - Drug- or toxin-induced (e.g., certain prescription diet drugs, illegal drugs like methamphetamines)
  - Associated with congenital heart disease (left-to-right shunts)
  - Connective tissue disorders (e.g., scleroderma, lupus, others)
  - HIV infection
  - Chronic liver disease (cirrhosis)
  - Eisenmenger syndrome (a congenital heart disease with large septal defect causing abnormal circulation and pulmonary hypertension)

- Group 2: Pulmonary hypertension caused by left-sided heart disease
  - Left-sided valvular disease (mitral valve or aortic valve disease)
  - Left ventricular failure

- Group 3: Pulmonary hypertension caused by lung disease
  - Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) such as emphysema
  - Interstitial lung disease such as pulmonary fibrosis
  - Sleep apnea and other sleep disorders
  - Long-term exposure to high altitudes

- Group 4: Pulmonary hypertension caused by chronic blood clots
  - Chronic thromboembolic disease (pulmonary emboli)

- Group 5: Pulmonary hypertension associated with other conditions with unclear reasons
  - Blood disorders
  - Multiorgan disorders (e.g., sarcoidosis)
  - Metabolic disorders (e.g., glycogen storage disease)
  - Tumors pressing against pulmonary arteries
